since_last: Since Wed midday: NVDA held $200 through both closes ($200.42); MU lost $900 at the bell ($891.88) but is reclaiming it today; Oracle — yesterday's risk card — answered the AI-capex question with a −12% gap.

cards.headline: Split tape: semis rip while software and adtech bleed
cards.body: SOXX +2.74% and SMH +2.07% against a flat SPY (+0.03%) — the rotation inside the AI universe is the story, not the index. Universe breadth is an even 45/83 green, with sector averages stretched from Equipment +2.6% to Platforms & AdTech −2.5% (1:20 PM ET, ~15-min delayed). TLT +0.31% — bonds calm.

cards.headline: ORCL −12.5%: record cloud growth, but the $70B capex bill spooks
cards.body: Oracle posted cloud revenue +93% and announced a $70 billion AI spending plan — and trades ~$176, down 12.5%. The market's new fault line: AI spenders get sold, AI suppliers get bought.
cards.watch: Acceptance below $175 leaves the post-earnings gap in control; back above $180 steadies the cloud group.

cards.headline: Equipment is the new leadership
cards.body: LRCX +7.1%, KLAC +8.5%, AMAT +5.9%, ASML +4.6%, ARM +5.4% — toolmakers lead the universe while Musk pitches a 'Terafab' factory to ASML ahead of the SpaceX IPO. Whoever wins the AI race, capex flows through the same equipment names — Oracle's $70B plan feeds them too.

cards.headline: Memory squeezes higher: DRAM ETF +5.6%, MU back above $900
cards.body: MU +2.6% to ~$915 — reclaiming the $900 level it lost at yesterday's close, with SanDisk +7.5% alongside. A close back above $900 turns yesterday's breakdown into a failed move, and failed moves often travel.
cards.watch: MU's close vs $900 — above = failed breakdown reversal.

cards.headline: NVDA glued to $200
cards.body: Third session pinned to the battleground: −0.2% at ~$200.1. Leadership rotated to equipment while the biggest name digests — constructive as long as the level holds.
cards.watch: $200 remains the structure line; a decisive break either way sets the next leg.

cards.headline: Funding source: adtech, China platforms and big software
cards.body: SNAP −4.5%, ADBE −4.5%, SAP −5.3%, BABA −4.7%, BIDU −3.7% — Apps & Data (−2.4%) and Platforms & AdTech (−2.5%) are where the semi rally is funded from. Risk lens: if SpaceX-IPO froth cools or Iran headlines re-escalate, the rotation unwinds from the semis side first.

disclaimer: Key Takeaways are generated for informational and educational purposes only and do not constitute investment advice, a recommendation, or an offer to buy or sell any security. Markets move fast — data may be delayed and levels can change. Always do your own research.
sources: Yahoo Finance delayed quotes via the Santro AI pipeline (83-ticker universe, DRAM holdings, context tape); site news feed; previous cycle = Wed midday block.
